_comment: WC2026 Round of 32, 2026-07-01, Mexico City Stadium. KNOCKOUT: no draw -- level after 90' -> extra time -> penalties, so the deliverable is who ADVANCES. Routes: Mexico WON Group A with a perfect nine points and ZERO goals conceded (2-0 South Africa, 1-0 South Korea, 3-0 Czech; GF 6 GA 0, rating 79) -- the HOST nation, defensively elite (three clean sheets), playing AT HOME in Mexico City. Ecuador qualified as a BEST THIRD from Group E (0-1 Ivory Coast, 0-0 Curacao, 2-1 Germany; 4 pts, GF 2 GA 2, rating 79) -- organized and defensive (low-scoring, GF 2) but capable of an upset (beat Germany 2-1 in MD3). Same rating (79=79), so on neutral ground this is a coin-flip; the differentiator is MEXICO'S HOME ADVANTAGE in Mexico City. KEY NUANCE: the altitude edge is largely NEUTRALISED because Ecuador is itself an altitude side (Quito ~2,850m) -- so Mexico's home advantage is crowd/familiarity, MODEST, not the big altitude swing it would be vs a sea-level team. Both sides are defensive and low-scoring -> a TIGHT, low-scoring tie (a 1-0/0-0/1-1 shape) with a real ET/penalty risk, like the round's other defensive ties. Mexico the SLIGHT favourite by home edge. Home = Mexico (REAL home, not neutral). Cards not modelled (referee TBD).

meta.stakes: Single-elimination: level after 90' -> extra time -> penalties. Equal-rated sides (79=79); Mexico the slight favourite via a MODEST home advantage (Mexico City crowd, but altitude is neutralised vs altitude-team Ecuador). Both defensive and low-scoring -> a tight, low-event tie with real ET/penalty risk.

home.name: Mexico
notes: Host, won Group A on a perfect nine with ZERO conceded (3 clean sheets). Elite defence: defense 0.85.
Efficient but not prolific attack (GF 6 vs a modest group). attack 1.05.
Home advantage in Mexico City baked into base_home 1.32 (crowd/familiarity; altitude neutralised vs Ecuador).

away.name: Ecuador
notes: Organized, defensive (GA 2); beat Germany 2-1 -> can pull an upset. defense 0.90.
Altitude side itself -> Mexico's altitude edge neutralised; only modest home gap. Tight game, live for ET/penalties.

_recal: First pass sat 4.5 pts below the market on Mexico (44.8% vs ~49%): I under-weighted the host's home edge. The market makes Mexico a clearer (if modest) favourite. Lifted MEX 1.32 -> 1.42 and trimmed ECU 0.92 -> 0.88 to align; still a tight, low-scoring tie.
